researchLabGuide.title: How to Use VibeLab
researchLabGuide.description: Start in Chat to describe your research idea. The agent will generate your pipeline and tasks through conversation. Then review and execute tasks here.
researchLabGuide.step1: Go to Chat and describe your research topic, goal, or paper idea. The agent will ask clarifying questions.
researchLabGuide.step2: The agent generates a research pipeline and tasks based on your conversation.
researchLabGuide.step3: Review your tasks here in Research Lab. Click Go to Chat on any task to execute it with the agent.
researchLabGuide.step4: Track progress across Ideation, Experiment, and Publication stages. Refine the brief and tasks as you go.
researchLabGuide.interfaceMap: Interface: Task list and progress on the left, file preview on the right. Click Go to Chat on any task to execute it.
researchLabGuide.readmeHint: For full process details, see README.md -> Usage Guide.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep1: Fill core constraints first (goal, boundaries, metrics). Incomplete inputs are acceptable for initial task generation.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep2: Click Apply Template + Generate Tasks to update the research brief and regenerate the task list.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep3: Use Use in Chat from the Pipeline Task List below in sequence, and keep writing clarified conclusions back to the research brief.

researchLabTaskBoard.editHint: Double-click task card or use Edit button

messages.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.

mainContent.selectProjectDescription: Select a project from the sidebar to start coding with Claude. Each project contains your chat sessions and file history.
mainContent.createProjectMobile: Tap the menu button above to access projects
mainContent.createProjectDesktop: Create a new project by clicking the folder icon in the sidebar

fileTree.name: Name
fileTree.checkProjectPath: Check if the project path is accessible
fileTree.tryDifferentSearch: Try a different search term or clear the search

step1.question: Do you already have a workspace, or would you like to create a new one?

existing.title: Existing Workspace
existing.description: I already have a workspace on my server and just need to add it to the project list

new.title: New Workspace
new.description: Create a new workspace, optionally clone from a GitHub repository

step2.existingHelp: Full path to your existing workspace directory
step2.newHelp: Full path to your workspace directory
step2.githubHelp: Optional: provide a GitHub URL to clone a repository
step2.githubAuthHelp: Only required for private repositories. Public repos can be cloned without authentication.
step2.tokenHelp: This token will be used only for this operation
step2.publicRepoInfo: Public repositories don't require authentication. You can skip providing a token if cloning a public repo.
step2.noTokensHelp: No stored tokens available. You can add tokens in Settings → API Keys for easier reuse.

step3.existingInfo: The workspace will be added to your project list and will be available for Claude/Cursor sessions.
step3.newWithClone: The repository will be cloned from this folder.
step3.newEmpty: The workspace will be added to your project list and will be available for Claude/Cursor sessions.

errors.selectType: Please select whether you have an existing workspace or want to create a new one
